prompt: Build a Multi-Org Data & Media Operations Hub that runs entirely on a local network to support district-level administration across multiple organizations and locations while also powering an on-prem playback experience for curated audio content. Users include a System Administrator who sets up the hierarchy of company/factory/store/warehouse equivalents (district/campus/building/room or operations center/site/stockroom), a Data Steward who maintains master data, an Operations Analyst who monitors KPIs and generates reports, and a Standard User who consumes approved content and views permitted records. In the Vue.js web interface, admins can create and browse the org tree, switch context by location, and see clear status chips for active/inactive master data versions; changes are drafted, reviewed, then activated so users always work against a single "effective" version. Master data maintenance covers style SKU, color, size, season, brand, supplier, and customer with fast search, duplicate warnings, and bulk import from local files; deactivating a record requires a reason and immediately hides it from new selections but preserves historical usage. The playback page provides cover art, a dynamic background theme, and synchronized lyrics: LRC files can be attached locally, parsed for timeline alignment, and displayed with line highlighting; when word-level timing is available it highlights each word, and when lyrics are missing it falls back to a simple "Lyrics not available" panel while keeping playback controls responsive. Users can search within lyrics and jump playback to the closest timestamp, with visible confirmation within 200 ms. An analytics center presents KPI tiles and trend charts with filters constrained by the user's city/department scope, and supports scheduled report generation to local export files (CSV/PDF) at set times like 06:00 AM daily, with clear "ready/failed" indicators and downloadable history. The backend uses Gin to provide REST-style APIs consumed by the decoupled Vue.js frontend over the local LAN, and persists all business data in MySQL with transaction-backed versioning and auditability. Data ingestion is implemented as a plugin-style connector framework that can read from local folders, network shares, or on-prem databases, supporting scheduled incremental loads, full backfills, and dependency-aware job orchestration with priority queues; each job writes checkpoints every 1,000 records so it can resume after interruption, and failures retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff capped at 10 minutes before requiring operator acknowledgement. Import mapping rules and field validations are configurable per source (for example, SKU must be 6–20 uppercase alphanumerics, season must match patterns like "SS2026," and supplier records require a US-format phone such as (555) 123-4567). Authentication is local username/password only, issuing JWT or server sessions with a 30-minute idle timeout and a 12-hour maximum session; role-based access controls apply down to feature and data scope (city/department/location), and every create/update/delete and sensitive read is written to immutable operation logs. Biometric data, when stored for on-prem identity workflows, is encrypted at rest using AES-256 and in transit using TLS on the internal network; keys are stored locally with rotation every 90 days and access restricted by least privilege. Sensitive fields are classified and masked by default (for example, showing only the last 4 characters), retention policies can auto-purge raw ingestion files after 30 days, and audit logs are traceable and deletable only by a dual-authorized administrator action with a recorded reason and timestamp. Integration endpoints support student records/academic administration/SSO-style federation only in offline-compatible modes (local directory sync and token validation without external calls), while webhook/message extension points operate strictly within the local network to notify downstream on-prem systems of completed imports, activated master data versions, and scheduled report completion.
